comment_text.sn42.8:1.6: This saying echoes Sūtrakṛtāṅga 1.5.1.4–5, but appears to misrepresent the Jain text. There, Mahāvīra says a person who “relentlessly kills movable and immovable beings for the sake of his own comfort, who injures them, who takes what is not freely given, who does not train at all in what is to be cultivated, the impudent sinner who, unsated, injures many beings will go to hell” (translation adapted from Jacobi). The closeness of the two passages is shown by the form: the first phrase speaks of the ill deeds that are done, while the second phrase in both cases uses the word bahuṇa/bahula, “frequently, repeatedly, usually”. The use of “relentlessly” (tivva, Pali tibba, Sanskrit tīrva) implies a violent and excessive action. As quoted in the Pali, the first phrase of Mahāvīra’s view suggests that anyone at all who kills will go to hell. This sense is conveyed through the emphatic distributive pronoun yo koci (“everyone who”), whereas the Jain text has the unemphatic je (“one who”). The Jain text restricts the distributive to one who does not train “at all” (kiṁci). Thus the thrust of the Jain passage is that one who frequently kills for their own pleasure, without even trying to do better, will go to hell. The sense of the Pali passage is both more severe and less coherent, as the first phrase implies that any killer goes to hell, while the second speaks of habitual action.
comment_text.sn42.8:7.6: The following passage shows a healthy response to one’s own ethical lapses, lost neither in guilt nor in denial, but acknowledging the mistake and determining to do better.
comment_text.sn42.8:11.1: These are the “ten ways of doing skillful deeds” (dasakusalakammapathā). There is a detailed explanation at MN 41:7.1.
comment_text.sn42.8:12.1: Established on skillful deeds of body, speech, and mind, they undertake the set of four meditations known in later traditions as either the “divine meditations” (brahmavihārā) or the “immeasurables” (appamaññā). These are four wholesome emotional states that can be developed as a basis for samādhi. They were evidently pre-Buddhist, although they have not been traced as a group in pre-Buddhist texts. However, they are shared with later non-Buddhist texts such as Yogasūtra 1.33 and the Jain Tattvārthasūtra 7.11. | “Love” (mettā) is a universal positive regard and well-wishing free of personal desires or attachments.
comment_text.sn42.8:12.3: The mind in jhāna is so powerful that it effectively overrides any limited kamma, including ordinary good or bad deeds. Unless they have committed serious crimes with a fixed kammic result such as matricide, etc., the meditator will be reborn in a Brahmā realm.
comment_text.sn42.8:13.1: “Compassion” (karuṇā) is the quality of empathy with the suffering of another or oneself and the wish to remove it.
comment_text.sn42.8:13.2: “Rejoicing” (muditā) is joyful celebration in the success of others or oneself, free of jealousy or cynicism.
comment_text.sn42.8:13.3: Equanimity (upekkhā) is literally “close watching”, not interfering but standing ready when needed. It is not indifference, which is why it emerges only at the end, after the positive emotions are developed.

root_text.sn42.8:1.1: Ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā nāḷandāyaṁ viharati pāvārikambavane.
root_text.sn42.8:1.2: Atha kho asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi nigaṇṭhasāvako yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho asibandhakaputtaṁ gāmaṇiṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.8:1.3: “kathaṁ nu kho, gāmaṇi, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desetī”ti?
root_text.sn42.8:1.4: “Evaṁ kho, bhante, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ deseti:
root_text.sn42.8:1.5: ‘yo koci pāṇaṁ atipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci musā bhaṇati sabbo, so āpāyiko nerayiko.
root_text.sn42.8:1.6: Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulaṁ viharati, tena tena nīyatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:1.7: Evaṁ kho, bhante, nigaṇṭho nāṭaputto sāvakānaṁ dhammaṁ desetī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:1.8: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati, tena tena nīyati’, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:2.2: yo so puriso pāṇātipātī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so pāṇamatipāteti, yaṁ vā so pāṇaṁ nātipātetī”ti?
root_text.sn42.8:2.3: “Yo so, bhante, puriso pāṇātipātī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo yaṁ so pāṇamatipāteti, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo yaṁ so pāṇaṁ nātipātetī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:2.4: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:3.2: yo so puriso adinnādāyī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so adinnaṁ ādiyati, yaṁ vā so adinnaṁ nādiyatī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:3.3: “Yo so, bhante, puriso adinnādāyī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya appataro so samayo, yaṁ so adinnaṁ ādiyati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so adinnaṁ nādiyatī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:3.4: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:4.1: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso kāmesumicchācārī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so kāmesu micchā carati, yaṁ vā so kāmesu micchā na caratī”ti?
root_text.sn42.8:4.2: “Yo so, bhante, puriso kāmesumicchācārī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo yaṁ so kāmesu micchā carati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so kāmesu micchā na caratī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:4.3: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:5.1: Taṁ kiṁ maññasi, gāmaṇi, yo so puriso musāvādī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, katamo bahutaro samayo, yaṁ vā so musā bhaṇati, yaṁ vā so musā na bhaṇatī”ti?
root_text.sn42.8:5.2: “Yo so, bhante, puriso musāvādī rattiyā vā divasassa vā samayāsamayaṁ upādāya, appataro so samayo, yaṁ so musā bhaṇati, atha kho sveva bahutaro samayo, yaṁ so musā na bhaṇatī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:5.3: “‘Yaṁbahulaṁ yaṁbahulañca, gāmaṇi, viharati tena tena nīyatī’ti, evaṁ sante na koci āpāyiko nerayiko bhavissati, yathā nigaṇṭhassa nāṭaputtassa vacanaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:6.1: Idha, gāmaṇi, ekacco satthā evaṁvādī hoti evaṁdiṭṭhi:
root_text.sn42.8:6.2: ‘yo koci pāṇamatipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko, yo koci musā bhaṇati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.3: Tasmiṁ kho pana, gāmaṇi, satthari sāvako abhippasanno hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.6: yo koci pāṇamatipāteti, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.7: Atthi kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito, ahampamhi āpāyiko nerayikoti diṭṭhiṁ paṭilabhati.
root_text.sn42.8:6.8: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
root_text.sn42.8:6.10: yo koci adinnaṁ ādiyati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.11: Atthi kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ ahampamhi āpāyiko nerayikoti diṭṭhiṁ paṭilabhati.
root_text.sn42.8:6.12: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
root_text.sn42.8:6.14: yo koci kāmesu micchā carati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayiko’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.15: Atthi kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:6.17: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
root_text.sn42.8:6.19: yo koci musā bhaṇati, sabbo so āpāyiko nerayikoti.
root_text.sn42.8:6.20: Atthi kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:6.22: Taṁ, gāmaṇi, vācaṁ appahāya taṁ cittaṁ appahāya taṁ diṭṭhiṁ appaṭinissajjitvā yathābhataṁ nikkhitto evaṁ niraye.
root_text.sn42.8:7.1: Idha pana, gāmaṇi, tathāgato loke uppajjati arahaṁ sammāsambuddho vijjācaraṇasampanno sugato lokavidū anuttaro purisadammasārathi satthā devamanussānaṁ buddho bhagavā.
root_text.sn42.8:7.2: So anekapariyāyena pāṇātipātaṁ garahati vigarahati, ‘pāṇātipātā viramathā’ti cāha.
root_text.sn42.8:7.6: Tasmiṁ kho pana, gāmaṇi, satthari sāvako abhippasanno hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:7.8: ‘bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena pāṇātipātaṁ garahati vigarahati, pāṇātipātā viramathāti cāha.
root_text.sn42.8:7.9: Atthi kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito yāvatako vā tāvatako vā.
root_text.sn42.8:7.10: Yo kho pana mayā pāṇo atipātito yāvatako vā tāvatako vā, taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.
root_text.sn42.8:7.11: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ.
root_text.sn42.8:7.12: Na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:7.13: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva pāṇātipātaṁ pajahati.
root_text.sn42.8:8.1: ‘Bhagavā kho anekapariyāyena adinnādānaṁ garahati vigarahati, adinnādānā viramathāti cāha.
root_text.sn42.8:8.2: Atthi kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.
root_text.sn42.8:8.3: Yaṁ kho pana mayā adinnaṁ ādinnaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.
root_text.sn42.8:8.4: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:8.5: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva adinnādānaṁ pajahati.
root_text.sn42.8:9.1: ‘Bhagavā kho pana anekapariyāyena kāmesumicchācāraṁ garahati vigarahati, kāmesumicchācārā viramathāti cāha.
root_text.sn42.8:9.2: Atthi kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.
root_text.sn42.8:9.3: Yaṁ kho pana mayā kāmesu micchā ciṇṇaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu.
root_text.sn42.8:9.4: Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:9.5: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva kāmesumicchācāraṁ pajahati, āyatiñca kāmesumicchācārā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:10.1: ‘Bhagavā kho pana anekapariyāyena musāvādaṁ garahati vigarahati, musāvādā viramathāti cāha.
root_text.sn42.8:10.2: Atthi kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā.
root_text.sn42.8:10.3: Yaṁ kho pana mayā musā bhaṇitaṁ yāvatakaṁ vā tāvatakaṁ vā taṁ na suṭṭhu, taṁ na sādhu. Ahañceva kho pana tappaccayā vippaṭisārī assaṁ, na metaṁ pāpaṁ kammaṁ akataṁ bhavissatī’ti.
root_text.sn42.8:10.4: So iti paṭisaṅkhāya tañceva musāvādaṁ pajahati, āyatiñca musāvādā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:10.5: Evametassa pāpassa kammassa pahānaṁ hoti. Evametassa pāpassa kammassa samatikkamo hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:11.1: So pāṇātipātaṁ pahāya pāṇātipātā paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:11.5: Pisuṇaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pisuṇāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:11.6: Pharusaṁ vācaṁ pahāya pharusāya vācāya paṭivirato hoti.
root_text.sn42.8:12.1: Sa kho so, gāmaṇi, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno paṭissato mettāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ mettāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.sn42.8:12.2: Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, balavā saṅkhadhamo appakasireneva catuddisā viññāpeyya;
root_text.sn42.8:12.3: evameva kho, gāmaṇi, evaṁ bhāvitāya mettāya cetovimuttiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya yaṁ pamāṇakataṁ kammaṁ, na taṁ tatrāvasissati, na taṁ tatrāvatiṭṭhati.
root_text.sn42.8:13.1: Sa kho so, gāmaṇi, ariyasāvako evaṁ vigatābhijjho vigatabyāpādo asammūḷho sampajāno paṭissato karuṇāsahagatena cetasā …pe…
root_text.sn42.8:13.3: Upekkhāsahagatena cetasā ekaṁ disaṁ pharitvā viharati, tathā dutiyaṁ, tathā tatiyaṁ, tathā catutthaṁ. Iti uddhamadho tiriyaṁ sabbadhi sabbattatāya sabbāvantaṁ lokaṁ upekkhāsahagatena cetasā vipulena mahaggatena appamāṇena averena abyāpajjena pharitvā viharati.
root_text.sn42.8:13.4: Seyyathāpi, gāmaṇi, balavā saṅkhadhamo appakasireneva catuddisā viññāpeyya;
root_text.sn42.8:13.5: evameva kho, gāmaṇi, evaṁ bhāvitāya upekkhāya cetovimuttiyā evaṁ bahulīkatāya yaṁ pamāṇakataṁ kammaṁ na taṁ tatrāvasissati, na taṁ tatrāvatiṭṭhatī”ti.
root_text.sn42.8:14.1: Evaṁ vutte, asibandhakaputto gāmaṇi bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn42.8:14.3: upāsakaṁ maṁ bhagavā dhāretu ajjatagge pāṇupetaṁ saraṇaṁ gatan”ti.

translation_text.sn42.8:1.1: At one time the Buddha was staying near Nāḷandā in Pāvārika’s mango grove.
translation_text.sn42.8:1.2: Then Asibandhaka’s son the chief, who was a disciple of the Jains, went up to the Buddha, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn42.8:1.3: “Chief, how does the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan teach his disciples?”
translation_text.sn42.8:1.4: “Sir, this is how the Jain Ñātika teaches his disciples:
translation_text.sn42.8:1.5: ‘Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.
translation_text.sn42.8:1.6: You’re led on by what you frequently live by.’
translation_text.sn42.8:1.7: This is how the Jain Ñātika teaches his disciples.”
translation_text.sn42.8:1.8: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain Ñātika says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.
translation_text.sn42.8:2.2: Take an individual who kills living creatures. If we compare periods of time during the day and night, which is more frequent: the occasions when they’re killing or when they’re not killing?”
translation_text.sn42.8:2.3: “The occasions when they’re killing are less frequent, while the occasions when they’re not killing are more frequent.”
translation_text.sn42.8:2.4: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain ascetic of the Ñātika clan says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.
translation_text.sn42.8:4.1: Take an individual who commits sexual misconduct …
translation_text.sn42.8:5.1: Take an individual who lies. If we compare periods of time during the day and night, which is more frequent: the occasions when they’re lying or when they’re not lying?”
translation_text.sn42.8:5.2: “The occasions when they’re lying are less frequent, while the occasions when they’re not lying are more frequent.”
translation_text.sn42.8:5.3: “‘You’re led on by what you usually live by’: if this were true, then, according to what the Jain Ñātika says, no-one would go to a place of loss, to hell.
translation_text.sn42.8:6.1: Take some teacher who has this doctrine and view:
translation_text.sn42.8:6.2: ‘Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.’
translation_text.sn42.8:6.3: And there’s a disciple who is devoted to that teacher.
translation_text.sn42.8:6.5: ‘My teacher has this doctrine and view:
translation_text.sn42.8:6.6: “Everyone who kills a living creature, steals, commits sexual misconduct, or lies goes to a place of loss, to hell.”
translation_text.sn42.8:6.21: They get the view: ‘I too am going to a place of loss, to hell.’
translation_text.sn42.8:6.22: Unless they give up that speech and that thought, and let go of that view, they will be placed in hell as if delivered there.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.1: But consider when a Realized One arises in the world, perfected, a fully awakened Buddha, accomplished in knowledge and conduct, holy, knower of the world, supreme guide for those fit for training, teacher of gods and humans, awakened, blessed.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.2: In many ways he criticizes and denounces killing living creatures, saying: ‘Stop killing living creatures!’
translation_text.sn42.8:7.6: And there’s a disciple who is devoted to that teacher.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.8: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces killing living creatures, saying: “Stop killing living creatures!”
translation_text.sn42.8:7.9: But I have killed living creatures to a certain extent.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.10: That’s not right, it’s not good,
translation_text.sn42.8:7.11: and I feel remorseful because of it.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.12: But I can’t undo what I have done.’
translation_text.sn42.8:7.13: Reflecting like this, they give up killing living creatures,
translation_text.sn42.8:7.14: and in future they don’t kill living creatures.
translation_text.sn42.8:7.15: That’s how to give up this bad deed
translation_text.sn42.8:8.1: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces stealing …’
translation_text.sn42.8:9.1: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces sexual misconduct …’
translation_text.sn42.8:10.1: ‘In many ways the Buddha criticizes and denounces lying, saying: “Stop lying!”
translation_text.sn42.8:10.2: But I have lied to a certain extent.
translation_text.sn42.8:10.3: That’s not right, it’s not good, and I feel remorseful because of it. But I can’t undo what I have done.’
translation_text.sn42.8:10.4: Reflecting like this, they give up lying, and in future they refrain from lying.
translation_text.sn42.8:10.5: That’s how to give up this bad deed and get past it.
translation_text.sn42.8:11.1: They give up killing living creatures.
translation_text.sn42.8:11.9: They give up ill will and malevolence.
translation_text.sn42.8:11.10: They give up wrong view and have right view.
translation_text.sn42.8:12.1: That noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of love to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of love to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.sn42.8:12.2: Suppose there was a powerful horn blower. They’d easily make themselves heard in the four quarters.
translation_text.sn42.8:12.3: In the same way, when the heart’s release by love has been developed and cultivated like this, any limited deeds they’ve done don’t remain or persist there.
translation_text.sn42.8:13.1: Then that noble disciple is rid of desire, rid of ill will, unconfused, aware, and mindful. They meditate spreading a heart full of compassion …
translation_text.sn42.8:13.2: They meditate spreading a heart full of rejoicing …
translation_text.sn42.8:13.3: They meditate spreading a heart full of equanimity to one direction, and to the second, and to the third, and to the fourth. In the same way above, below, across, everywhere, all around, they spread a heart full of equanimity to the whole world—abundant, expansive, limitless, free of enmity and ill will.
translation_text.sn42.8:13.4: Suppose there was a powerful horn blower. They’d easily make themselves heard in the four quarters.
translation_text.sn42.8:13.5: In the same way, when the heart’s release by equanimity has been developed and cultivated like this, any limited deeds they’ve done don’t remain or persist there.”
translation_text.sn42.8:14.1: When he said this, Asibandhaka’s son the chief said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn42.8:14.3: From this day forth, may the Buddha remember me as a lay follower who has gone for refuge for life.”

variant_text.sn42.8:7.12: pāpaṁ kammaṁ → pāpakaṁ kammaṁ (bj); pāpakammaṁ (sya-all, km, pts1ed, mr)
